action.reasoning: Observation: the tiny LoRA run completed under memory constraints, but val grounding_score is still 0.0 with many missing/invalid outputs, meaning the issue is now mostly output-format learning/parsing rather than pipeline breakage. Analysis: training on concatenated prompt+target without masking prompt tokens makes the model waste capacity copying the prompt; additionally target format using bbox when many labels are points may hurt. The fastest high-upside fix is to (1) mask labels so loss is only on assistant target tokens, (2) train only point outputs by converting every sample to a canonical <point>x y</point> target using target_point if available else bbox center, which aligns with the metric and is easier to learn, and (3) improve parser to catch more generated variants. Keep subset modest to fit time, then validate locally and submit eval if nonzero. Decision: patch model/train for point-only SFT with prompt masking, add stronger parsing, run a 1-epoch subset training and local val scoring end-to-end, and submit eval if results improve.
